I’ve spent over 5 years showing up and creating Solana content to get here.

I tweeted every single day about Solana and life, regardless of how I felt, regardless of the noise, and regardless of market sentiment.

Through bull markets, bear markets, chaos, doubt, and silence. I stayed. I kept posting.

Thank you to everyone who took a second out of their day to like, comment, rt, dm, or simply stand beside me on this journey.

Your support was literally fuel whenever I doubted. I will forever be grateful.

Thank you. I love you all so much.

tweets.full_text:
I received an unexpected call today.

One of my girlfriend’s besties was filming a vlog with a content creator in Spain, and while they were talking, the guy casually mentioned that he started his spiritual journey after reading tweets from a random X account called Solana Sensei and talking to him. 

My girlfriend’s bestie knows I am Sensei, so she instantly video called my girlfriend asking for me, and I ended up talking to a loyal og follower while I was on my way to the gym. We were both equally shocked, and he had no idea I even spoke Spanish.

He told me that I got him into his spiritual journey and inspired him to create more spiritual content instead of just self-development content.

He could have said he discovered Solana or crypto through me, but the fact that he said he began a spiritual journey because of Solana Sensei made it 1000x more meaningful to me. That truly made my day.

He inspired me just as much, and now I feel called to bring back my og spiritual tweets to the timeline. I think we need more love right now.

What a wholesome moment.

Very grateful for life.

Anchor v1.0.0 is live

v1.0.0 is the first stable major release of Anchor. Here's what's new:

- No more Solana CLI dependency
- Surfpool & LiteSVM as defaults for faster testing
- @ anchor-lang/core — new home for the TS package
- Migration<From, To> for account schema upgrades
- Lifecycle hooks in Anchor.toml
- avm self-update- declare_program! instruction parser
- Duplicate mutable accounts rejected by default

Installation instructions 👇

pertanyaan yang paling sering masuk: "kalau gua install Meridian dan langsung running, langsung profit?"

jawaban singkatnya: ngga.

framework-nya bisa gua build. tapi edge dari Meridian bukan dari framework-nya. edge-nya adalah dari lo sendiri.

tujuan agent ini adalah mindah otak dan flow lo dalam DLMM ke dalam agent itu. strategi, preferensi, cara milih token, cara nentuin range, cara lo exit. semua itu yang bikin agent kalian jadi kalian

pastel hasilin $102 dalam sehari. prune $153 dengan 76% win rate. mereka bukan pemula yang baru install terus untung. 

mereka udah jago DLMM di Meteora, dan yang mereka lakuin adalah transfer ilmu mereka ke dalam agent ini. dari situ agent-nya jalan sendiri dengan cara pikir yang sama.

gua tanya ke orang-orang yang dapet $50 ke atas per hari dari agent ini. rata-rata mereka main dengan modal 2 SOL per posisi, maksimal 3 posisi jalan bersamaan.

kalau lo belum ngerti DLMM sama sekali, pelajarin dulu sebelum nyoba Meridian. bukan karena agent-nya susah, tapi karena lo ga bisa transfer ilmu yang lo belum punya.

data.translation:
most common question that comes in: "if i install Meridian and just run it, instant profit?"

short answer: no.

i can build the framework. but the edge from Meridian isn't from the framework. the edge is from you yourself.

the goal of this agent is to transfer your brain and flow in DLMM into the agent itself. strategy, preferences, way of picking tokens, way of determining range, way you exit. all that is what makes your agent become you

pastel made $102 in a day. prune $153 with 76% win rate. they're not newbies who just installed and then profited.

they're already pros at DLMM on Meteora, and what they do is transfer their knowledge into this agent. from there the agent runs on its own with the same mindset.

i asked people who get $50 and up per day from this agent. on average they play with 2 SOL capital per position, max 3 positions running at the same time.

if you don't understand DLMM at all yet, learn it first before trying Meridian. not because the agent's hard, but because you can't transfer knowledge you don't have yet.

data.translation:
Lots of people asking how to get started so they can understand what your agents are doing in Meteora.

The answer's simple: you gotta understand DLMM first before trying to run an agent. The pastel I quote-tweeted is already 2 years LP in Meteora. That's why he can consistently profit from Meridian—'cause he already knows how to "drive" his agent right.

Here's the roadmap:

- Start by learning the basics of Meteora and DLMM
- Ask around about Meteora and learn together with the community—join dc @MeteoraIDN 
- Join Meteora's bootcamp, held once a month

From there, you'll figure out how to find good pools, set entry and exit rules, and how to train your agent with the knowledge you've already got.

The agent's able to run. But what makes it profitable is the person behind it.

tweets.full_text:
80085-sol, three times. $35, $42, $149. same token, same strat, just kept farming it.

narrative was great last night and the token survived, so i kept going. volume was sitting at 200k-350k per 5 minutes, TVL was low, pool was on 5% base fee. that combination is exactly what you want for spot tight. 

pool link in the comments if you want to farm this too.

the strat is spot -10%, single side sol. the exit rule is strict: when price touches about half of my position and starts going out of range.

i withdraw and reposition if it's still pumping. no waiting, no hoping it comes back in range. out, reassess, redeploy.

that's why the third position held for 6 minutes and made $149. the first two were quick cycles, the third one caught a bigger move and i let it run until the same exit rule triggered.

but this strat needs real self-control. the moment you start thinking "let me hold a bit more" or "it'll come back in range" you've already broken the logic.

the whole edge is in executing the exit exactly when the condition hits, not when it feels right.

if you don't trust yourself to exit on the rule, this strat won't work for you. it's that simple.

@MeteoraAG @met_lparmy

“The French community is cooking at the moment. Like, we I think we have a huge community now. We've been mainly on the discord. It's called the diggers. Discord. “So I was so happy. I was like, okay, this is getting good. Let's do some streams.
